subject: Geography
chapter: The World Population Distribution, Density and Growth

definitions.definition: The number of people living in a particular area or country at a given time. It includes all the people in the area, including infants, children, women, men, and the elderly.
The number of people living in a unit area, usually measured in square kilometers. It is calculated by dividing the total population by the total area.
The way in which the population is spread over the earth's surface in terms of density and concentration. It can be uneven, even, or regular.
The increase in the number of people living in a country or area over time, usually measured by the birth rate and death rate.
The number of live births per 1,000 people in a year. It is an indicator of the population growth rate.
The number of deaths per 1,000 people in a year. It is an indicator of the population decline rate.
The difference between the birth rate and death rate. It is the rate at which the population grows naturally.
The average number of daughters a woman would have in her lifetime, assuming a stable population and current age-specific fertility and mortality rates.
The level of fertility at which a population replaces itself from one generation to the next, without any increase or decrease.
The number of live births per 1,000 people per year, without considering the age or sex of the population.
The number of deaths per 1,000 people per year, without considering the age or sex of the population.
The ratio of people in a population who are not in the working age group (children and elderly) to those who are.
The way in which the population is divided into different age groups, such as children, working-age people, and the elderly.
The movement of people from rural areas to cities and towns in search of employment and better living conditions.
The movement of people from cities and towns to rural areas in search of a more peaceful and natural way of life.
The movement of people from one place to another, either within a country or internationally.
The movement of people from one place to another within a country, either to another city, town, or rural area.
The movement of people from one country to another, either to live and work or to visit as tourists.
